mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS relative to the prior 3 months lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which raises S&P 500 forward 20-day returns when the cut is part of an easing cycle rather than an inflation shock.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls at least 25 bps over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if average 20-day return is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high or rising FEDFUNDS rate above the neutral range compresses equity valuation multiples and tightens credit, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate was 5.33% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than periods below the threshold, or if the sign of the average effect is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects weakening household income growth and slower final demand, which leads equities to reprice lower over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-surprise episodes are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the cumulative average forward return is non-negative across any 4-quarter window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption demand and revenue growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through better near-term earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month growth in RSAFS does not correspond to higher subsequent 20-day SPX returns than flat or declining sales growth, or if the sign reverses in a 3-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, the economy is expanding through real supply rather than inflation, supporting margins and lowering policy pressure, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint episodes of rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL do not outperform other periods in 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and a slower housing wealth channel, which precedes weaker equity returns over the next week as cyclical growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend housing starts are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal has no negative edge over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS slows while UNRATE rises, labor income growth is weakening faster than headline employment levels suggest, and equities should underperform over the next 20 trading days as consumer-demand and earnings expectations are marked down.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls below 0.1% and UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration signal does not beat either series alone in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered instances show positive average returns over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad final-demand deceleration, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because both output and nominal spending are losing momentum.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 1% and RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-growth signal does not produce lower 60-day SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if average forward returns are non-negative in a rolling 3-year sample.
